dailyNews.title: ‘Not in a complete deadlock': Mediators rush to revive US-Iran talks ahead of ceasefire deadline — report
dailyNews.description: Days after the US-Iran talks failed in Pakistan, mediators are now trying to revive the negotiations before the two-week ceasefire ends on 21 April. Turkish and Egyptian foreign ministers spoke to their Pakistani counterpart on 12 April, after which they spoke to Witkoff and Iran's Araghchi.

dailyNews.description: The image, posted on Truth Social, appeared to be A.I.-generated and depicts the president as a divine leader healing the sick.

dailyNews.description: The judge said President Trump had not “plausibly alleged” that The Journal published the article with actual malice.

dailyNews.title: U.S. begins blockade of Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: The U.S. blockade would apply to "any and all Ships trying to enter, or leave, the Strait of Hormuz," President Donald Trump said.

dailyNews.title: Trump blasts Pope Leo for criticism of U.S. foreign policy
dailyNews.description: Leo, the first U.S.-born pope who hails from Chicago, has condemned Trump's war effort with Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump threatens 50% tariffs on China as report suggests plans for arms shipment to Iran
dailyNews.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to impose a 50% tariff on China, after a report on Sunday that Beijing was preparing a weapons shipment to Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says US Navy Will Blockade Hormuz in Major Escalation
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump has announced a United States military blockade on the critical Strait of Hormuz after peace talks between Vice President JD Vance and Iranian negotiators in Pakistan broke down over the weekend. U.S. Central Command said it will prevent vessels entering or departing Iranian ports but won’t block non-Iranian ships. NBC’s Richard Engel reports for TODAY.

dailyNews.title: Trump Ally Viktor Orbán Concedes Defeat in Hungary Election
dailyNews.description: Voters in Hungary ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, marking a stunning defeat for the close ally of both President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin. The winner, Péter Magyar, has pledged to rebuild Hungary’s relationships with the European Union and NATO.
